---
pagetitle: "High 0.05 | prove your health product works"
description: "High 0.05 turns clinical data into evidence that regulators, buyers and investors accept: captured cleanly, analysed honestly, presented clearly."
page-layout: full
toc: false
title-block-banner: false
listing:
  - id: listing-home
    contents:
      - "posts/datah/papers"
      - "posts/datah/medical_datactor"
    sort: "date desc"
    type: grid
    max-items: 3
    image-height: 180px
    categories: false
    feed: true
---

::: {.hero}
::: {.hero-inner}
[Clinical evidence &amp; biostatistics · a CUAMM spin-off]{.eyebrow}

# Prove your health product [works]{.u}.

::: {.lede}
High 0.05 turns clinical data into evidence that regulators, buyers and
investors accept. Captured cleanly, analysed honestly, and presented so anyone
in the room can read it.
:::

::: {.hero-actions}
[Get in touch](jobs.qmd){.btn-primary-cta}
[See how it works](#how){.btn-secondary-cta}
:::
:::
:::


::: {.section .section-alt}
::: {.section-head}
## From raw data to proof, in three steps {#how}

Each step is where teams get stuck. Here is what the fix looks like.
:::
:::

```{=html}
<div class="section" style="padding-top:0; padding-bottom:4.5rem;">
<div class="pipeline">

  <div class="stage">
    <div class="stage-num">01 · Capture</div>
    <h3>Data that lands clean</h3>
    <p>Validation and automatic calculations built into data capture, so errors are caught at entry, not months later.</p>
    <div class="demo demo-capture">
      <div class="dc-row">
        <span class="dc-label">Systolic BP</span>
        <span class="dc-val">
          <span class="v v-bad">320 <span class="dc-chip bad">out of range</span></span>
          <span class="v v-good">128 <span class="dc-chip good">valid</span></span>
        </span>
      </div>
      <div class="dc-row"><span class="dc-label">Weight</span><span class="dc-val">72 kg</span></div>
      <div class="dc-row"><span class="dc-label">eGFR (auto)</span><span class="dc-val"><span class="dc-auto">74 mL/min</span></span></div>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="stage">
    <div class="stage-num">02 · Analyse</div>
    <h3>Analysis that holds up</h3>
    <p>Study design, survival, regression and effect sizes, run properly and read in context, not just "p &lt; 0.05".</p>
    <div class="demo demo-chart">
      <svg viewBox="0 0 360 160" role="img" aria-label="Two outcome curves compared over time">
        <line class="axis" x1="20" y1="10" x2="20" y2="138"></line>
        <line class="axis" x1="20" y1="138" x2="346" y2="138"></line>
        <polyline class="line2" points="20,24 72,34 122,48 182,66 242,88 302,104 346,112"></polyline>
        <polyline class="line1" points="20,26 72,44 122,64 182,92 242,112 302,126 346,132"></polyline>
      </svg>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="stage">
    <div class="stage-num">03 · Prove</div>
    <h3>Results anyone can read</h3>
    <p>Raw model output turned into a clear table a board or a regulator understands in seconds.</p>
    <div class="demo demo-prove">
      <div class="layer raw">
<pre>Coefficients:
             Estimate Std.Err     p
(Intercept)    0.5405  4.2458  0.90
Exposure       0.0177  0.0028  0.00036
Age            0.1500  0.0306  0.00001</pre>
      </div>
      <div class="layer tbl">
        <table class="pv-table">
          <thead><tr><th>Characteristic</th><th>Effect</th><th>95% CI</th><th>p</th></tr></thead>
          <tbody>
            <tr><td>Exposure</td><td>0.02</td><td>0.01, 0.02</td><td>&lt;0.001</td></tr>
            <tr><td>Age</td><td>0.15</td><td>0.09, 0.21</td><td>&lt;0.001</td></tr>
          </tbody>
        </table>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>

</div>
</div>
```


::: {.section}
::: {.trust}
Trusted with clinical data and analysis by <b>health-tech companies, hospitals
and research teams across Europe.</b>
:::
:::


::: {.section .tight}
::: {.trust}
High 0.05 is a spin-off of <b>Medici con l'Africa CUAMM</b>, which has run health
programs across sub-Saharan Africa since 1950. That is where the standard comes
from: decisions backed by evidence, not opinion.
:::
:::


::: {.section .section-alt}
::: {.section-head}
## Insights

Short, practical notes on statistics, data and health tech.
:::

::: {#listing-home}
:::
:::


::: {.section}
::: {.cta-final}
## Have a product you need to prove?
Tell us what you are building and where the evidence gap is. If it involves
data, statistics or clinical validation, it has almost certainly come up before.

[Get in touch](jobs.qmd){.btn-primary-cta}
:::
:::
